ai.description: The link text "Click here" is generic and provides no information about the destination when read out of context by screen readers.
The link has an empty aria-label attribute which overrides the visible text, resulting in no accessible name being announced by assistive technologies.

ai.issueType: Label in Name mismatch on link
ai.description: The visible link text "Open in new tab" is not contained within the accessible name "Homepage (opens in new tab)", failing to match the label visible on screen with the name spoken by speech recognition users.
The heading hierarchy skips from h2 to h4, bypassing h3 and creating a confusing document outline for screen reader users.
The email input has no associated label or aria-label, making it unidentifiable to screen reader users.
The email input collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill.
The text input for full name has no label or aria-label, so screen readers cannot convey its purpose.
The input for full name lacks the autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from filling the user's name automatically.
The telephone input has no label or aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
The telephone input lacks the autocomplete attribute, preventing autofill of the user's phone number.
The textarea has no associated label or aria-label, making it unidentifiable to screen reader users.
The logo image lacks an alt attribute, preventing screen readers from conveying its content to users.
The page contains two nav elements. This nav lacks an aria-label, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from the secondary legal navigation.
The "Menu" button controls expandable navigation content but lacks the aria-expanded attribute, preventing screen reader users from knowing the current state.
The email input in the newsletter form lacks the autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from autofill assistance.

ai.issueType: Div with role img missing accessible name
ai.description: The div has role="img" but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute, so screen readers cannot convey the image content.

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
lighthouse.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Select elements do not have associated label elements.
lighthouse.description: Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. [Learn more about the `select` element](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/select-name).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.

lighthouse.issueType: Document does not have a main landmark.
lighthouse.description: One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. [Learn more about landmarks](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/landmark-one-main).

lighthouse.issueType: Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names.
lighthouse.description: Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. [Learn more about accessible names](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label-content-name-mismatch).
lighthouse.element: <a href="/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="Homepage (opens in new tab)">

lighthouse.issueType: `<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers.
lighthouse.description: Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. [Learn more about table headers](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/td-has-header).
